questions.question: The graph of y = sin(x) is shifted right by π/4 units and stretched vertically by a factor of 2. Write the equation of the resulting function.
questions.explanation: A rightward shift by π/4 replaces x with (x - π/4). A vertical stretch by factor 2 multiplies the entire function by 2. Combine: y = 2sin(x - π/4).

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 5, 7, and 8. Find the measure of the angle opposite the side of length 7 (round to the nearest tenth of a degree).
questions.explanation: Use the Law of Cosines: b² = a² + c² − 2ac·cos(B). Here 7² = 5² + 8² − 2(5)(8)cos(B), so 49 = 89 − 80cos(B). Thus cos(B) = 0.5, giving B ≈ 57.1°.

questions.question: What is the period of the function y = cos(3x)?
questions.explanation: For y = cos(bx), the period is 2π/|b|. Here b = 3, so period = 2π/3.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 40°, angle B = 65°, and side a = 10. Use the Law of Sines to find the length of side b (round to the nearest tenth).
questions.explanation: By the Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So 10/sin(40°) = b/sin(65°). Thus b = 10·sin(65°)/sin(40°) ≈ 10(0.906)/0.643 ≈ 15.0.

questions.question: The function y = tan(x) has a vertical asymptote at x = π/2. At what x-value does the next vertical asymptote occur?
questions.explanation: Vertical asymptotes of tan(x) occur where cos(x) = 0, which happens at x = π/2 + nπ for integer n. The next asymptote after π/2 is at π/2 + π = 3π/2.

questions.question: A triangle has sides a = 6, b = 9, and c = 10. Find cos(C), where C is the angle opposite side c.
questions.explanation: Use the Law of Cosines: c² = a² + b² − 2ab·cos(C). So 100 = 36 + 81 − 2(6)(9)cos(C), which gives 100 = 117 − 108cos(C). Thus cos(C) = 17/108 = -1/4 (after simplification).
